errors.sentence: She don't like coffee in the morning.
errors.reason: Third person singular requires "doesn't"

errors.sentence: He goed to the store yesterday.
errors.reason: "Go" has an irregular past tense: "went"

errors.sentence: They was playing football all day.
errors.reason: Plural subjects use "were" not "was"

errors.sentence: I have went to Paris three times.
errors.reason: Present perfect uses past participle: "have been"

errors.sentence: She is more smarter than her brother.
errors.reason: Don't use "more" with comparative "-er" forms

errors.sentence: The childrens are playing outside.
errors.reason: "Children" is already plural — no need for "-s"

errors.sentence: He can speaks three languages fluently.
errors.reason: After modal verbs, use the base form of the verb

errors.sentence: I am agree with your opinion completely.
errors.reason: "Agree" is not used with "be" — it stands alone

errors.sentence: She suggested me to go to the doctor.
errors.reason: "Suggest" takes a that-clause, not an object + infinitive

errors.sentence: He is knowing the answer to every question.
errors.reason: "Know" is a stative verb — not used in continuous form

errors.sentence: I have seen her yesterday at the mall.
errors.reason: Use simple past with specific past time markers like "yesterday"

errors.sentence: The informations are very useful for us.
errors.reason: "Information" is uncountable — it has no plural form

errors.sentence: She gave to me a beautiful birthday present.
errors.reason: With indirect objects, no preposition is needed before the person

errors.sentence: If I will see him, I will tell him.
errors.reason: In first conditional, use simple present in the if-clause

errors.sentence: He told that he was feeling very sick.
errors.reason: "Tell" requires an object (tell someone); "say" does not

errors.sentence: She must to study harder for the exam.
errors.reason: Modal verbs are followed directly by the base verb — no "to"

errors.sentence: I am used to get up early every morning.
errors.reason: "Used to" + gerund means being accustomed to something

errors.sentence: He drived to work because the bus was late.
errors.reason: "Drive" has an irregular past tense: "drove"

errors.sentence: Each of the students have their own locker.
errors.reason: "Each" is singular and takes a singular verb

errors.sentence: I look forward to hear from you soon.
errors.reason: "Look forward to" is followed by a gerund (-ing form)

errors.sentence: The movie was bored, so we left early.
errors.reason: Use "-ing" adjectives for things that cause the feeling

errors.sentence: She doesn't knows how to swim yet.
errors.reason: After "doesn't," use the base form of the verb

errors.sentence: They didn't went to school this morning.
errors.reason: After "didn't," use the base form of the verb

errors.sentence: My brother is more older than me by two years.
errors.reason: Don't use "more" with comparative "-er" forms

errors.sentence: He has been living here since five years.
errors.reason: Use "for" with durations; "since" with specific points in time

errors.sentence: I am believing that he is innocent.
errors.reason: "Believe" is a stative verb — not used in continuous form

errors.sentence: We have less students this year than last year.
errors.reason: Use "fewer" for countable nouns; "less" for uncountable

errors.sentence: Despite of the rain, we went for a long walk.
errors.reason: "Despite" is never followed by "of" — use it alone

errors.sentence: She is enough old to make her own decisions.
errors.reason: "Enough" comes after adjectives, not before them

errors.sentence: He suggested to go to the new Italian restaurant.
errors.reason: "Suggest" is followed by a gerund, not an infinitive

errors.sentence: I wish I can travel around the whole world.
errors.reason: "Wish" for unreal present situations uses past tense

errors.sentence: The news are very shocking and unexpected today.
errors.reason: "News" is uncountable and always takes a singular verb

errors.sentence: She asked me where do I live now.
errors.reason: Indirect questions use statement word order, not question order

errors.sentence: He is one of the most tallest player on the team.
errors.reason: Don't use "most" with superlative "-est" forms

errors.sentence: I have been to London last summer with my family.
errors.reason: Use simple past with specific past time markers

errors.sentence: She made me to clean my entire room before dinner.
errors.reason: After "make" someone, use the bare infinitive (no "to")

errors.sentence: Neither the teacher nor the students was happy.
errors.reason: With "neither...nor," the verb agrees with the nearest subject

errors.sentence: He denied to steal the money from the safe.
errors.reason: "Deny" is followed by a gerund, not an infinitive

errors.sentence: I did not found my keys anywhere in the house.
errors.reason: After "did not," use the base form of the verb

errors.sentence: She has been married with him for ten years now.
errors.reason: The correct preposition is "married to," not "married with"

errors.sentence: My mother cook delicious food every day.

errors.sentence: I am studying English since two years.

errors.sentence: She enjoy to read books in her free time.

errors.sentence: He study every night before going to bed.

errors.sentence: He write in his journal every night before bed.
errors.reason: Third person singular requires "-s" on the verb

errors.sentence: I have less apples than oranges in my bag.

errors.sentence: He enjoy to read comic books on weekends.

errors.sentence: I am agree with what the teacher said today.

errors.sentence: They was at the library yesterday evening late.

errors.sentence: She don't likes vegetables at all unfortunately.

errors.sentence: I have did all my chores already today.

errors.sentence: He is more intelligent than his classmates are.
